_comment: Template: Deploy agents defined in a gitagent-standard git repository.
_comment2: Supports two overlay strategies: init-container (default) and build-image.
_comment3: See https://github.com/open-gitagent/gitagent for the gitagent directory spec.
name: GitAgent from Repository
description: Deploy one or more agents whose soul, rules, skills, tools, hooks, and scheduler are all defined in a gitagent-standard git repository. Supports private repos via SSH keys or HTTPS tokens. Uses Docker image layers (build-image) or Kubernetes init-containers (init-container) to overlay the repository files into the agent runtime.

team.name: gitagent-team
team.description: Agents sourced from a gitagent-standard git repository

agents._comment2: Kubernetes adds an alpine/git init container that clones the repo at pod startup.
agents._comment3: The cloned files are mounted at mountPath (/agent) for the OpenClaw runtime.
agents.description: Agent whose definition lives in a git repository (init-container strategy)

git._comment_auth: For private repos: use tokenSecret (HTTPS) or sshKeySecret (SSH)

source._comment_include: Optional: restrict which files/dirs are copied. Default: all standard gitagent files.

agents._comment_identity: With gitagent source, SOUL.md → identity.personality, RULES.md → hard constraints.
agents._comment_identity2: You can still set identity here to override or supplement the git-sourced content.

agents._comment: Strategy 2: build-image — bakes git content into Docker image layers at build time.
agents._comment2: Run `shadowob-cloud build` to generate and build the image before deploying.
agents._comment3: Better for production: no git access needed at runtime, reproducible layers.
agents.description: Agent whose definition is baked into a custom Docker image layer at build time

git._comment_token: tokenSecret can be a K8s Secret name or an env var like GITHUB_TOKEN for HTTPS private repos

agents._comment_image: After `shadowob-cloud build`, set image to the built tag.

agents._comment: SSH-authenticated private repo — for repos that require SSH key access.
agents._comment2: Create a K8s secret first: kubectl create secret generic git-ssh-key --from-file=ssh-privatekey=~/.ssh/id_ed25519
agents.description: Agent from a private git repository authenticated via SSH

agents._comment: Monorepo example — multiple agents from the same repository, different subdirectories.
agents._comment2: All agents share the same git.url; each points to its own agents/<name> subdirectory.
agents.description: Agent A from a monorepo — agents/agent-a subdirectory
Agent B from the same monorepo — agents/agent-b subdirectory

agents._comment: Local dev path example — reference a local gitagent directory during development.
agents._comment2: At deploy time, shadowob-cloud reads the local files and enriches the agent config.
agents._comment3: In production, switch source.git to a git URL.
agents.description: Agent loaded from a local gitagent directory (development only)

_gitagent_directory_structure._comment: For reference: the standard gitagent repository layout (from https://github.com/open-gitagent/gitagent)

layout.agent.yaml: Required. Agent manifest: name, version, model, tools, skills, compliance.
layout.SOUL.md: Required. Agent personality and identity definition.
layout.RULES.md: Optional. Hard constraints injected into the system prompt.
layout.skills/: Optional. Skill definitions (skills/{name}/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter).
layout.memory/: Optional. Memory strategy (memory/MEMORY.md, memory/runtime/ for live state).
